user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The rear of the car is clearly visible from a direct rear angle, showing the bumper area with visible deformation and damage.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 shows the rear of a white sedan with significant deformation of the rear bumper cover, including a visible dent/crush across the bumper surface consistent with the customer's claim.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images show the front of a white car with the front bumper clearly visible. img_1 shows visible damage on the front bumper; img_2 shows the same front bumper area in apparent good condition.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows the front bumper with significant damage including visible scrapes, paint loss, and deformation near the headlight area — far more than a 'light scratch.' img_2 shows the same front bumper appearing undamaged. The visible damage in img_1 contradicts the user's claim of only a light scratch on the front bumper.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the windshield with extensive cracking/shattering radiating from an impact point on the driver's side. img_2 shows the same windshield from a different angle with no visible damage, but img_1 alone is sufficient to evaluate the claim.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a windshield with a clear impact point on the lower-left (driver's side) area with multiple radiating cracks spreading across the glass, consistent with a stone impact. The damage is more extensive than 'small' but the claim of a crack spreading from an impact point is visually supported.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The side mirror is clearly visible in img_1 with sufficient detail to assess its condition, satisfying REQ_CAR_GLASS_LIGHT_MIRROR and REQ_GENERAL_OBJECT_PART.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows the side mirror with a clearly shattered/cracked mirror glass and the mirror housing appears displaced/loose, consistent with being clipped. The mirror is visibly broken and not sitting properly.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images clearly show the rear of the car with the rear bumper visible from an angle where deformation can be assessed.
claim_status_justification: Both img_1 and img_2 show a silver car from the rear with a visible dent/deformation on the rear bumper area, consistent with the customer's claim of rear bumper damage.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image shows a side view of the car; the headlight is not visible, so the claimed part cannot be inspected.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows the side of the car (mirror, door, fender) but the headlight is not in frame, so a crack cannot be confirmed or ruled out.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_2 clearly shows the door panel with a visible dent; img_1 is blurry but shows the car from a different angle.
claim_status_justification: img_2 shows a clear dent on the door panel of the car, matching the customer's claim. img_1 is blurry but shows the car from a different angle.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image is a stock photo (Vecteezy watermark) showing a heavily damaged/wrecked car in a forest, not the customer's vehicle. The hood is not visible from an angle that would allow assessment of a scratch. The image is not an original photo of the claimed vehicle.
claim_status_justification: img_1 is a Vecteezy stock photo of a wrecked car in a forest, not the customer's vehicle. The hood is open and not visible from an angle to assess a scratch. No usable original evidence of the claimed scratch on the hood.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The laptop screen is clearly visible with an obvious spiderweb crack pattern radiating from an impact point, consistent with the user's claim.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 shows the laptop open with a clearly visible spiderweb crack pattern on the screen glass, with an impact point in the upper-center area and radiating fracture lines across the display. This directly supports the customer's claim of a cracked screen.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: No visual evidence provided in the provider output to assess the laptop hinge condition.
claim_status_justification: No visible evidence available in the provided images to confirm or deny the hinge damage.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image clearly shows the laptop keyboard with visible water droplets and pooling water, sufficient to assess the claimed condition.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows water droplets on the keyboard keys and a puddle on the palm rest, consistent with the user's report of water spilled over the keys.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image 1 shows the laptop from above without clear view of the claimed damaged corner; Image 2 is too blurry to confirm or rule out a dent.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows the laptop lid but not the claimed damaged corner in detail; img_2 is extremely blurry and does not provide clear evidence of a dent on the corner.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The laptop screen is clearly visible in img_1 with sufficient detail to inspect for cracks or shattering.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a MacBook Pro with a clearly shattered screen, including a prominent spider-web impact pattern radiating from a central point on the right side and additional cracks across the display surface. This matches the customer's claim of a shattered screen.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the laptop trackpad and surrounding body area at close range, sufficient to inspect for physical damage.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 shows the trackpad and surrounding palm rest/body area in close-up. The surface appears intact with no visible cracks, dents, scratches, or deformation. A white hand-drawn circle annotation highlights an area but no actual damage is visible within or around it.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image clearly shows the package corner with visible crushing damage, matching the claimed part and issue.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a cardboard box with a visibly crushed corner where the cardboard is bent inward and flaps are deformed, consistent with the user's claim.

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the package exterior with visible torn and crushed damage on the seal/tape area; img_2 shows an intact reference box. The claimed part (seal side) is visible enough to evaluate the claim.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a cardboard box with clearly torn cardboard and ripped tape along the seal/center seam, consistent with the customer's claim that the seal side was torn and the parcel appeared opened. img_2 shows a separate intact box and does not contradict the claim.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The package side is clearly visible with sufficient detail to assess staining and warping consistent with water exposure.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a cardboard box with prominent dark staining and streaking patterns on the side panel, along with visible warping/sagging of the cardboard in the affected area. These visual cues are consistent with water damage and a wet-looking stain as claimed.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the opened box interior filled with crumpled newspaper/paper filler; no ordered product is visible among the filler. img_2 shows the closed exterior with label and tape seal.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows the opened box containing only crumpled paper/newspaper filler material with no ordered product visible inside. This supports the customer's claim that the contents are missing.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image shows cans (product contents) rather than the shipping box exterior that the user claims was crushed, so the claimed damage cannot be verified.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows cans with nutrition labels but no shipping box exterior; the crushed box the user describes is not visible in the image.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images show the package exterior and seal area clearly. img_1 shows torn seal tape at the corner; img_2 shows an intact sealed box.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows torn 'TAMPER EVIDENT VOID' tape at the package corner/seal area, consistent with the customer's claim of a torn seal. img_2 shows a different, intact sealed box which may not be the same package.
